title: 8. Dry Run and Performance
description: How to use Dry Run, streaming chunk values, VRAM modes, and performance profiles without guessing.

steps.title: Why Dry Run Exists
steps.body: Dry Run measures the active configuration instead of relying on generic advice. It is most useful after the core app starts and before you spend time hand-tuning chunking.

steps.title: Choose the Configuration First
steps.body: Select avatar engine, VRAM mode, TTS backend, model, and stream mode before arming Dry Run. The recommendation only applies to the setup that was measured.

steps.title: Start Conservatively
steps.body: For MuseTalk, begin with Very Low VRAM. If that works well, test higher modes one at a time and save profiles that are stable on your hardware.

steps.title: Arm Dry Run
steps.body: Arm Dry Run here. Hands-Free can let the app generate test replies automatically, but you can also answer manually if you want more control.

steps.title: Initialize After Arming
steps.body: Initialize the system after Dry Run is armed. That lets the measurement include startup and early reply behavior for the exact configuration.

steps.title: Review the Recommendation
steps.body: When Dry Run completes, read the summary before applying anything. If the run was interrupted or the model behaved strangely, rerun it rather than saving a noisy profile.

steps.title: Save Useful Profiles
steps.body: Performance profiles are local machine knowledge. Keep one profile for each setup you actually use, such as MuseTalk Very Low VRAM with Chatterbox streaming.
